item.title: Ep. 105 Mathias Guillemette: Racing Milton World Cup
item.description:
What's  going on guys, welcome back to another episode of Coffee and Van Chats on the Out of Collective Network. This week we chatted with Elimination mad man Mathias Gulliemette! We chat all things about the Milton World Cup and what's to come for Mathias and Cycling Canada.

item.title: Ep. 102 Chris Schroeder: Diamond Factory Racing
item.description:
What is going on guys, welcome back to another episode of Coffee and Van Chats, a part of the Out of Collective. This week we chatted with Chris Schroeder about his transition from triathlon to gravel and how he wants a great well supported program for the 2023 season and the foreseeable future. item.title: Ep. 101 Brian Abers: "It was a tainted supplement..."
item.description:
What is going on guys, welcome back to another episode of Coffee and Van Chats, a part of the Out of Collective. This week we chatted with Brian Abers about his recent positive test with USADA which was reduced after finding trace amounts of the banned product in his supplements. Brian is a cycling coach by trade as well as a 50-55 + masters track athlete.

Don’t forget to leave us a 5 star review...or one star, it's up to you but we love feedback.

Follow our host & show:

item.title: Ep. 97 Kiel Reijnen Pro Cyclist, Sailor, and Adventurer
item.description:
What is going on guys, welcome back to another episode of Coffee and Van Chats, a part of the Out of Collective. This week we chatted with Kiel Reijnen. Kiel is so much more than a pro cyclist and has an interesting story which lands him into cycling! Kiel is a father, a sailor, a fisherman and an all around adventurer!

Don’t forget to leave us a 5 star review...or one star, it's up to you but we love feedback.

item.title: Ep. 95 The Vegan Cyclist: Who is Tyler Pearce?
item.description:
What is going on guys, welcome back to another episode of Coffee and Van Chats, a part of the Out of Collective. This week we chatted with The Vegan Cyclist - Tyler Pearce. Tyler's life is a wild one and he is known to have worn many hats in life! We sat down to see how Tyler became the Vegan Cyclist. Don’t forget to leave us a 5 star review...or one star, it's up to you but we love feedback. item.title: Ep. 94 Bryan Larsen: Injury that changed the narrative.
item.description:
What is going on guys, welcome back to another episode of Coffee and Van Chats, a part of the Out of Bounds Network. This week we chatted with Bryan LArsen who is an elite level pro cyclist who was in an awful accident in 2019 which rewrote his entire narrative. Bryan chats about what he went through before and after the accident and how he processed the change for the better.

Don’t forget to leave us a 5 star review...or one star, it's up to you but we love feedback.

item.title: Ep. 92 Rasmus Pedersen Danish Olympian and World Champion
item.description:
What is going on guys, welcome back to another episode of Coffee and Van Chats, a part of the Out of Bounds Network. This week we chatted with Olympic silver medalist and World Champion - Rasmus Pedersen about the 2021 Tokyo Olympic games and the drama that underlies the TP along with what the plans are for him and the Danish team going into Paris!

item.title: Ep. 91 Rob Gitelis - CEO of FACTOR, Track bike in the works, How they survived COVID!
item.description:
What is going on guys, welcome back to another episode of Coffee and Van Chats, a part of the Out of Bounds Network. This week we sit down with probably one of the smartest men in the cycling industry - Rob Gitelis who is the CEO of FACTOR Bicycles. We chat about what it was like owning a company during COVID and how it is currently affecting the company with new things in the pipeline along with the collaborations with brands and athletes alike and how that makes FACTOR one of the highest performance bike brands in the industry!

Don’t forget to leave us a 5 star review...or one star, it's up to you but we love feedback.

item.title: Ep. 90 Ed Clancy OBE: Olympic Mindset
item.description:
What is going on guys, welcome back to another episode of Coffee and Van Chats, a part of the Out of Bounds Network. This week is an exciting one chatting with 4 time Olympian 3 time gold medalist on the track, Ed Clancy OBE. We chat about Ed’s new ventures as well as what it took to stay in the sport of track cycling for so long!

Don’t forget to leave us a 5 star review...or one star, it's up to you but we love feedback.

item.title: Ep. 89 Christian Vande Velde: Racing Track to Road to NBC Commentating
item.description:
What is going on guys, welcome back to another episode of Coffee and Van Chats, a part of the Out of Bounds Network. This week we chat with Christian Vande Velde, known currently for his cycling commentary on NBC for the Vuelta and Tour De France. In this episode we sit down and chat about Vande Velde’s start in cycling all the way to the man behind the camera.

item.description:

item.title: Ep. 77 Glenn Catchpole: VeloBike NZ
item.description:
What is going on guys, welcome back to another episode of Coffee and Van Chats, a part of the Out of Bounds Network. On this week's episode we chat with Glenn Catchpole about the start of VeloBike and how he went from a CAD design to a concept that went on to win a few Olympic Medals in 2021. Don’t forget to leave us a 5 star review...or one star, it's up to you but we love feedback. item.description:

This week we chatted to cyclocross legend Katie Compton about her recent positive doping test and she gets a chance to tell her truth. item.title: Ep. 71 Olympic Recap with Olympic Medalist Emma White!
item.description:
What is going on guys welcome back to another episode of Coffee and Van Chats where we sit down with Olympic Bronze Medalist Emma White. Emma comes from a CX and road background riding with some of the best in the world such as Cannondale CX and Rally Cycling!   

In this episode we chat what it was like to win a medal and how it felt to be at the Olympic Games!

item.description:

item.title: Ep. 47 Cody Townsend Pro Skier, Mountaineer and Bikepacker
item.description:
Welcome back to another episode of Coffee and Van Chats. On this episode we chat with Pro Skier Cody Townsend about the recent bikepacking trip he did to the base of Mt. Hood in Oregon. Cody bikepacked 1,000 miles to the base of Mt. Hood and then hike up to the top to ski one of the best lines of North America for his series "The Fifty" which is based on Cody skiing 50 of the best lines in North America which comes from the book "The Fifty Classic Ski Descents of North America" which is written by Chris Davenport and Penn Newhard and designed by Art Burrows. We chat all about his start into skiing and his goals for the project and how he had too adjust for COVID.


This episode is Sponsored by:

Spot

Bike Hardcore

Meier Skies

item.title: Ep. 46 Chats on the new ODA Program with USA Cycling with CEO Rob DeMartini and more!
item.description:
Welcome back to another episode of Coffee and Van Chats! This one is an intense podcast as it brings on the CEO of USA Cycling Rob DeMartini, Performance director Jim Miller, Cyclocross Manager Jesse Anthony, Apex Coaching Neal Henderson, Legion of LA Ama Nsek, Ex professional cyclist and member of the USA National Team Andrea Dvorak, and finally ex professional from Hincapie Cycling Evan Bybee. On this episode we chat about the elephant in the room of cycling of the new ODA program which has acquired some bad press due to it is hefty price tag of $10,500. 

So sit back and enjoy as we hash out all the questions and get the real story behind the Olympic Development Academy so you can create your own opinion.

This episode is sponsored by Spot

item.description:
Welcome back to another episode of Coffee and Van chats - my name is John Croom and on this episode we chat with Kevin Vermaerke who is the young American talent that comes from Axeon Hagens Berman. We chat about what it is like signing to a World Tour Team in the middle of a pandemic with such uncertainty of the future and no real results for 2020 what was the tools that help Kevin get a contract to his dream team for 2021. 

Kevin has been named to be one of the riders to upset the world tour in 2021 by Velonews, what do you guys think? Let us know by reaching out to info@johncroomcycling.com.


Check out 

Kevin Vermaerke Instagram

Team DSM

Check out the sponsor of this podcast: 

Spot

item.title: Ep. 41 Gus Morton of Thereabouts/Outskirts/PSYO Podcast
item.description:
Welcome back everyone to another episode of Coffee and Van Chats. On this episode we chat with Angus Morton. Angus has spent many years in the pro peloton before heading back to university to chase film school only to find himself back on two wheels chasing some unfinished business which lead to the start of Thereabouts and Outskirts. We chat with Angus on how he decide to start Thereabouts along with some of he most interesting characters he has met on the road.

item.title: Ep. 40 Magnus Sheffield World Record Holder and signs to Rally for 2021!
item.description:
Welcome back everyone to another episode of Coffee and Van Chats. On this episode we chat with Magnus Sheffield about his recent signing with Rally Pro Cycling along with the process of him breaking the UCI Junior Pursuit World Record by riding a blistering time of a 3:06! We dive in to the technology involved for a world record, what it's like signing for a pro cycling team during a pandemic. 



This episode is sponsored by Spot

item.title: Ep. 32 Scott Law of LEGION opens up about mental health in cycling!
item.description:
Hey guys welcome back to another episode of Coffee and Van Chats on this episode we chat with Scott Law of Legion of LA about how he found the love of cycling and how that affected his mental health. We also chat war stories from races such as Tulsa Tough and more!   

Cycling is a tough and lonely sport - and this episode dives in to the mental side and how it affected Scott, so please sit, back and enjoy. Know if you're not okay - REACH OUT! I am here for you and someone is there for you.


Check out Bike Hardcore

CODE: CROOM

item.title: Ep. 30 Sean Gardner jumps on the podcast as the EVEREST WORLD RECORD HOLDER!
item.description: Welcome back to another episode of Coffee and Van Chats - Sean Gardner is the first cyclist to break the 7 hour barrier doing the everesting challenge. Over quarantine the Everesting Challenge has become quite popular bringing out the likes of Phil Gaimon, Lachlan Morton, Alberto Contador and much more but Sean is the first cyclist to put that record below 7 hours! Listen in on how Sean had to hold 300+ watts for the 52 laps to smash this record!

Dante come out of California riding for some of the biggest fixie crit and road crit teams on the west coast such as CNCPT and LEGION OF LA. Dante has recently moved to Boulder, CO working for HUNT Wheels and we chat about his new gig, where it all started for Dante, the current state of affairs and where he sees his cycling career going! 


Please sit back relax and enjoy and make sure you SUBSCRIBE!

item.description:
Hey guys welcome back to another episode of Coffee and Van Chats - I am John Croom and on this episode of the podcast we chat with Gavin Hoover of Elevate KHS and Team USA. Gavin recently went to UCI Track Cycling Worlds where he would get 7th in the Omnium during Olympic year and it was his first Worlds in the Omnium. Gavin also rides professional on the road with Elevate KHS being apart of some of the biggest crit lead outs the country has seen! So please sit back relax and enjoy!

item.title: Ep. 21 Jake Sitler of Floyd's of Leadville chats about how CBD can be good for the athletes!
item.description:
What is going on guys welcome back to another episode of Coffee and Van Chats. My name is John Croom and I chat with Ex-Pro Cyclist and now business man Jake Sitler of Floyds of Leadville. Jake has been apart of some of the biggest US pro teams and chats about his transition from being a pro cyclist to being apart of one of the biggest CBD company's the sport has to offer! Jake was hit by a car before deciding to part ways with the pro cycling profession but Jake made the best out of a bad situation by creating big business dreams. Please sit back and relax and enjoy this episode! 


You can check out Floyds of Leadville here!

item.title: Ep. 20 Ian Boswell from World Tour to Gravel Adventure racing with Wahoo Frontiers!
item.description:
Welcome back to another episode of Coffee and Van Chats my name is John Croom and on this episode we chat with Ex-World Tour Pro Ian Boswell. Boswell was a professional cyclist for almost ten years riding or teams such as Trek Livestrong (Axeon Hagens Berman), Team Sky, and Katusha! Ian Boswell had a very scary crash which had him wondering if it was worth staying in the world tour or retiring when Wahoo came to him with an opportunity which would still have him riding his bike for a living. Ian is now a podcast host for Wahoo called Breakfast with Boz.


So sit back, relax and enjoy the episode!


This episode is brought to you by BeetRoot Pro and Chamois Butt'r

item.title: Ep. 17 Vincent De Haitre chats training for 2021 Summer Olympics while also training for 2022 Winter Olympics?!
item.description:
Hey guys welcome back to another episode off Coffee and Van Chats - we bring you some of the most interesting personalities the cycling has to offer! On this episode we chat with Vincent De Haitre. Vincent is currently 2 time winter Olympian in speed skating for Canada and has recently switched focus to the Team Pursuit in hope to crack into the 2020 Olympic Games. Vincent got 4th at the 2020 UCI Track Cycling World Championships in the kilometer time trial. 


This episode is brought to you by: Chili Technology

When you go to www.chilitechnology.com, you can take $150 off both the chiliPAD and OOLER with code CROOM.

item.title: Ep. 13 Dan Bigham on helping Denmark toward Olympic 2021 Gold Dreams!
item.description:
Welcome back to another episode of Coffee and Van Chats - On this episode we chat with Team Pursuit legend and probably the most controversial cyclist Dan Bigham. Along with being a team pursuit legend - when it comes t aerodynamics Dan knows it and has reinvented the way we look at being aero. Dan started and road for the british squad HUUB WattBike a squad built up of a bunch of misfits in the British Cycling Federations eyes. The squad would later go on to being a dream team leading to a few World Cup wins over some of the World best teams, even the British National Squad! 

Dan and I chat everything from Dan's UCI trade team HUUB Wattbike, his contract to work with Denmark for the 2021 Olympic Games Campaign and the controversial limited opportunities from British Cycling Federation. So please sit back, relax and enjoy this episode! 


This episode is brought to you by: Contravans and BeetRoot Pro


Huub WattBike Documentaries

item.description:
Hey guys welcome back to another episode of Coffee and Van Chats on this episode we chat with Peter Stetina. Pete road for Trek Segafredo before coming a gravel pioneer and running his own training program. We chat about his new van he just got along with his bike packing adventure to get said van and we chat about good beer and his new campaign FKT (Funest Known Time).
So sit back relax and enjoy!
This Episode is brought to you by: BeetRoot Pro

item.title: Ep. 11 Alison Tetrick Gravel Queen and Scientist?!
item.description:
Hey guys welcome back to another episode of Coffee and Van Chats. My name is John Croom.  On this episode of Coffee and Van Chats we chat with Gravel Cycling Queen Alison Tetrick. Alison chats with us about her Dirty Kanza 200 win, her unfortunate crash and the seriousness of TBI injuries, along with women inclusion in the sport and the fact that she is a scientist! So please sit back relax and enjoy! 

 If you liked this episode please make sure you give it a like as well as subscribe!   

Also if you want to check out the audio version head on over to:   https://www.youtube.com/channel/UCenWCMPCDaUTaGC510EwRBA?view_as=subscriber  

Coffee and Van Chats is brought to you by:   Contravans  

This episode is also sponsored by:   Orange Mud

item.title: Ep. 10 Phil Gaimon was my teammate for a day.
item.description: DISCLAIMER: Please understand this podcast was shot 2 weeks before Lachlan Morton would go on a shatter the Everest Record. Welcome back to my channel and podcast. On this episode of Coffee and Van Chat - I sit down in my van and chat with ex world tour pro/ strava KOM hunter and my short lived teammate Phil Gaimon. I have watched Phil as I entered the sport of cycling and then I heard he was possibly going to join us on the track. Unfortunately Phil would gone on to have a horrible crash and miss out on our 2019 World Cup campaign chasing the 2020 Olympic Games which we would fall short of. Phil and I chat about racing in TTown (Trexlertown), the Everest record, BBQ in Nashville and much more! Phil has an extensive pro cycling back ground which has brought him great success today! Again - sit back, relax and enjoy! Phil is known for his series worst retirement ever and his YouTube Channel and his series Phil Gaimon Worst Retirement ever can be found here: https://www.youtube.com/channel/UCqI9HyfLvodz7bXFCI3PU2g

item.title: Ep. 9 Kerry Werner eats sourdough on the podcast!
item.description:
Welcome back to another episode of Coffee and Van chats! My name is John Croom and if you haven't already please be sure to subscribe to the channel. The guest for this episode is Kerry Werner! Kerry has seen huge success in his cyclocross career along with some successes on the road and MTB! We chat about everything from his European cyclocross campaign all the way to his RV living/ van life and sourdough bread baking! Please sit back relax and enjoy! Kerry's youtube channel: https://www.youtube.com/channel/UCCJapudlF3KDpTlrzyr75Ow
Some of Kerry's Sponsors are: Shimano Kona Clif bar Eliel Maxxis Tires WD40 Bike BikeFlights and much more!

item.title: Ep. 8 Steve Cullen (Butchbox Cycling) drops wisdom!
item.description: Welcome back to my channel and podcast - my name is John Croom and on this episode of Coffee and Van Chats with Chat with Steve Cullen of Butcherbox Cycling. Steve has a huge passion for American Crit racing and been a big part of a team that take part in USA Crits which has evolved into one of the most exciting under dog teams around! Steve now is apart of the sports management side of Butcherbox cycling and bringing a whole new name to American pro criterium cycling! So please sit back, relax and enjoy! Be sure to check out Steve and his team on instagram @butcherboxcycling and www.fasturdays.com Again thank you for watching and be sure to subscribe!

item.title: Ep. 7 Chloe Dygert chats UCI Worlds TT and 2020 Olympics
item.description:
Hey Guys welcome back to my channel - If you don't know me my name is John Croom and on this episode of Coffee and Van Chats we chat with multiple time world champion in road and track cycling Chloe Dygert! I am really excited for this podcast because Chloe and I catch up on racing and training and just chat about some of the cool times we had on the road along with our love for cookies! So please sit back, relax and enjoy!
This Episode is brought to you by:
Orange Mud Contravans Switchback Coffee

item.title: Ep. 6 Mark Satkiewicz (SBT GRVL/SBT VRTL)
item.description:
On this weeks episode we chat with Mark Satkiewicz. Mark is the co founder of SBT GRVL. We chat with Mark about SBT Gravel cancelling along with the releasing the new SBT VRTL . Along with all that we dive into Mark past in high level industry marketing jobs which eventually brings him to being apart of one of the biggest gravel races in the country. Also - the video for this podcast for some reason got deleted in the conversion which is a bummer...so this will only be available on the pre-existing podcast platforms. So Sit back relax and enjoy.
How does SBT VRTL work and how can I participate?Register for SBT VRTL ASAP. It is FREE and invite your friends! Easy to register real quick right HERE Choose your distance (GREEN 37 miles, RED 64 miles, BLUE 103 miles or BLACK 144 miles) Choose your route (your own or one that we suggest) Decide who you’re riding with solo or with a few friends Sign up for the SBT GRVL Strava club and SBT VRTL event to easily see who else is participating and share with your friends!
Ride on August 16! This is not intended to be a big group ride or a race! Social distance, be smart and have fun! Adhere to all CDC and Government Covid-19 guidelines in your area and be safe. Take all of the snacks and tools that you will need. These rides are all self-supported Upload your ride to your Run Signup account when you are done
What are perks for the riders? SWAG. By donating $1 or more you are entered to win swag from our sponsors leading up to and on the day of the event. We have incredible partners and great products to give away.
Guaranteed entry to SBT GRVL 2021. If you are not currently deferred for SBT GRVL, you will have the opportunity to guarantee your ability to register for 2021 by donating $50 or more to any of our designated advocacy partners.
Where are the routes? You can always use your own route no matter where you live. In addition, we have identified 34 cities across the US where we will provide suggested routes to make it easier for you to complete one of the four distances. If you are in one of the cities below, you can find suggested routes on our website that links to Ride with GPS starting in July. You can use the routes on your own, or with a few friends starting any time on August 16th, your call. If you’re not in one of these cities, no problem, you may complete any of the four distances in your hometown and on the route of your choice.
Cities with suggested routes coming in July Albuquerque, Atlanta, Asheville, Austin, Boise, Boston, Boulder, Burlington, Colorado Springs, Dallas, Denver, Fort Collins, Golden Grand Rapids, Gainesville, Houston, Indianapolis, Kansas, City Knoxville, Little Rock, Los Angeles, Milwaukee ,Minneapolis, New York City, Park City Philadelphia, Portland, Salt Lake City, San Diego, San Francisco, Petaluma, Seattle, Steamboat Springs, Tucson
This Episode is brought to by: Contravans Switchback Coffee Orange Mud

item.title: Ep. 4 Nick Gould Chats gravel during COVID!
item.description: Hey guys welcome back to channel - today we interview Nick Gould. Gould is a professional mountain biker from the Durango area who has recently moved with his family to the Colorado Springs area! During this pandemic Nick has be managing a business, his training, stealing strava KOMs as well as prettying for his first child! Gould is in the first year of his new professional gravel team Roedo Lab Factory Team based out Colorado! Sit back, relax and enjoy!
